steps.title: Use complementary angles
steps.content:
In a right triangle, the two non-right angles are complementary. The problem states that $$A+B=90^\circ$$, so
$$B=90^\circ-A$$

steps.title: Match sine and cosine
steps.content: The cosine of an angle equals the sine of its complement. Therefore $$\cos B=\cos(90^\circ-A)=\sin A$$ Since $$\sin A=\frac{\sqrt{7}}{4}$$, the same value equals $\cos B$. The answer is A.
